title: Osmothamnus pallidus
taxonomy: Osmothamnus pallidus is classified within the family Fabaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as legumes. This classification places it among other woody legumes. Further classification within the genus Osmothamnus positions it as part of a unique and relatively small group of plants. The species is native to Western Australia, where it can be found growing in a variety of habitats. Its classification as a legume indicates its ability to form symbiotic relationships with nitrogen-fixing bacteria.

constituents: Osmothamnus pallidus is a plant species native to South Africa. The active constituents of Osmothamnus pallidus are primarily iridoid glycosides, including aucubin and catalpol, which have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
